docString:
Default configuration. Defines `proxyType` and `proxyTypeEquiv` in the namespace
of the inductive type. Uses `Unit`, `PLift`, `Sigma`, `Empty`, and `Sum`.

ref.pp:
/-- Default configuration. Defines `proxyType` and `proxyTypeEquiv` in the namespace
of the inductive type. Uses `Unit`, `PLift`, `Sigma`, `Empty`, and `Sum`. -/
def default (indVal : InductiveVal) : ProxyEquivConfig
    where
  proxyName := indVal.name.mkStr "proxyType"
  proxyEquivName := indVal.name.mkStr "proxyTypeEquiv"
  mkCtorProxyType := defaultMkCtorProxyType
  mkProxyType := defaultMkProxyType

where

docString:
Generates a proxy type for the inductive type and an equivalence from the proxy type to the type.

If the declarations already exist, there is a check that they are correct.

Helper function for `proxy_equiv% type : expectedType` elaborators.

Elaborate `type` and get its `InductiveVal`. Uses the `expectedType`, where the
expected type should be of the form `_ Ôëâ type`.

ref.pp:
/-- Helper function for `proxy_equiv% type : expectedType` elaborators.

Elaborate `type` and get its `InductiveVal`. Uses the `expectedType`, where the
expected type should be of the form `_ Ôëâ type`. -/
def elabProxyEquiv (type : Term) (expectedType? : Option Expr) : TermElabM (Expr ├Ś InductiveVal) := do
  let type ÔćÉ Term.elabType type
  if let some expectedType := expectedType? then 
    let equivType ÔćÉ Term.elabType (ÔćÉ `(_ Ôëâ $(ÔćÉ Term.exprToSyntax type)))
    unless ÔćÉ isDefEq expectedType equivType do
      throwError "Could not unify expected type{(indentExpr expectedType)}\nwith{indentExpr equivType}"
  let type ÔćÉ Term.tryPostponeIfHasMVars type "In proxy_equiv% elaborator"
  let type ÔćÉ whnf type
  let .const declName _ := type.getAppFn |
    throwError "{type} is not a constant or constant application"
    return (type, ÔćÉ getConstInfoInduct declName)

docString:
The term elaborator `proxy_equiv% ╬▒` for a type `╬▒` elaborates to an equivalence `╬▓ Ôëâ ╬▒`
for a "proxy type" `╬▓` composed out of basic type constructors `Unit`, `PLift`, `Sigma`,
`Empty`, and `Sum`.

This only works for inductive types `╬▒` that are neither recursive nor have indices.
If `╬▒` is an inductive type with name `I`, then as a side effect this elaborator defines
`I.proxyType` and `I.proxyTypeEquiv`.

The elaborator makes use of the expected type, so `(proxy_equiv% _ : _ Ôëâ ╬▒)` works.

For example, given this inductive type
```
inductive foo (n : Nat) (╬▒ : Type)
  | a
  | b : Bool Ôćĺ foo n ╬▒
  | c (x : Fin n) : Fin x Ôćĺ foo n ╬▒
  | d : Bool Ôćĺ ╬▒ Ôćĺ foo n ╬▒
```
the proxy type it generates is `Unit ÔŐĽ Bool ÔŐĽ (x : Fin n) ├Ś Fin x ÔŐĽ (_ : Bool) ├Ś ╬▒` and
in particular we have that
```
proxy_equiv% (foo n ╬▒) : Unit ÔŐĽ Bool ÔŐĽ (x : Fin n) ├Ś Fin x ÔŐĽ (_ : Bool) ├Ś ╬▒ Ôëâ foo n ╬▒
```

ref.pp:
/-- The term elaborator `proxy_equiv% ╬▒` for a type `╬▒` elaborates to an equivalence `╬▓ Ôëâ ╬▒`
for a "proxy type" `╬▓` composed out of basic type constructors `Unit`, `PLift`, `Sigma`,
`Empty`, and `Sum`.

This only works for inductive types `╬▒` that are neither recursive nor have indices.
If `╬▒` is an inductive type with name `I`, then as a side effect this elaborator defines
`I.proxyType` and `I.proxyTypeEquiv`.

The elaborator makes use of the expected type, so `(proxy_equiv% _ : _ Ôëâ ╬▒)` works.

For example, given this inductive type
```
inductive foo (n : Nat) (╬▒ : Type)
  | a
  | b : Bool Ôćĺ foo n ╬▒
  | c (x : Fin n) : Fin x Ôćĺ foo n ╬▒
  | d : Bool Ôćĺ ╬▒ Ôćĺ foo n ╬▒
```
the proxy type it generates is `Unit ÔŐĽ Bool ÔŐĽ (x : Fin n) ├Ś Fin x ÔŐĽ (_ : Bool) ├Ś ╬▒` and
in particular we have that
```
proxy_equiv% (foo n ╬▒) : Unit ÔŐĽ Bool ÔŐĽ (x : Fin n) ├Ś Fin x ÔŐĽ (_ : Bool) ├Ś ╬▒ Ôëâ foo n ╬▒
```
-/
@[term_parser 1000]
public meta def proxy_equiv : Lean.ParserDescrÔťŁ :=
  ParserDescr.nodeÔťŁ `Mathlib.ProxyType.proxy_equiv 1022
    (ParserDescr.binaryÔťŁ `andthen (ParserDescr.symbolÔťŁ "proxy_equiv% ") (ParserDescr.catÔťŁ `term 0))

/-- Elaborator for `proxy_equiv%`. -/
@[term_elab proxy_equiv]
def elab_proxy_equiv : Elab.Term.TermElab := fun stx expectedType? =>
  match stx with
  | `(proxy_equiv% $t) => do
    let (type, indVal) ÔćÉ elabProxyEquiv t expectedType?
    let config : ProxyEquivConfig := ProxyEquivConfig.default indVal
    ensureProxyEquiv config indVal
    mkAppOptM config.proxyEquivName (type.getAppArgs.map some)
  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax

value.pp:
:= fun stx expectedType? =>

    ensureProxyEquiv config indVal

  | _ => throwUnsupportedSyntax
